If the flight is delayed, I'll ask about my connection. If I miss it, I'll ask them to rebook me.

Good evening, welcome to the check-in desk. May I see your passport, and where are you flying tonight?
Good evening. Here's my passport. I'm flying to Lyon, with a layover in Paris.
Perfect, let me check you in. Are you checking a bag, or is that carry-on only?
Just this carry-on. But if it's too big for the overhead bin, will I have to check it at the gate?
It looks fine to me. But if the flight is full, the gate agent may check it for free. Now, do you have a seat preference?
Yes -- I'd like an aisle seat, please, if there's one left. I like to stretch my legs on a long flight.
You're in luck -- I have one aisle seat near the front. One more thing: if you have a long layover, you might need a transit visa. How long is your stop in Paris?
Only about two hours, so I think I'm fine. If it were longer, I'd check the rules. When does boarding start?
Boarding starts at gate B14 in about an hour. Listen for your boarding group. And if there's a gate change, just check the board -- it happens a lot here.
Thank you so much. So: aisle seat, gate B14, and if it changes I'll check the board. Have a good evening!
I'd like an aisle seat, please.
Is this flight on time?
If my flight is delayed, will I miss my connection?
Could you check my bag if it doesn't fit in the overhead bin?
Do I need a transit visa for the layover?

Good afternoon, and welcome to Terminal Four. This is a boarding announcement for flight nine zero four, with service to New York, John F. Kennedy. Please note that we have had a gate change. This flight will now depart from gate B twenty-two, not gate B fourteen as printed on your boarding pass. Again, that is a new gate: B twenty-two. If you need help finding it, any gate agent in a blue jacket will point you the right way. We will begin boarding in about twenty minutes. If you have a tight connection in New York, please come to the desk now and let us know, and we will do our best to help you. We will board by group, starting with passengers who need a little more time and our priority boarding members. When your boarding group is called, please have your boarding pass and your passport ready. Thank you for your patience, and we look forward to welcoming you on board.

Ladies and gentlemen, this is the final boarding call for flight twenty-two to Paris, Charles de Gaulle, now boarding at gate B twenty-two. All remaining passengers should make their way to the gate immediately, as the doors will close in ten minutes. If your name is on the standby list, please come to the desk. We have just released two seats, and we will call you in order. A quick reminder: only one carry-on and one small personal item are allowed in the cabin. If your bag does not fit in the overhead bin, our gate agent will check it through to Paris for free, and you can pick it up at baggage claim when you arrive. Passengers connecting in Paris, your next gate will be on the screen when you land, so there is no need to worry now. Once again, this is the final call for flight twenty-two to Paris. Please have your boarding pass ready as you come down the jet bridge. Thank you.
